headline: Claude dominates AI agent development as healthcare systems evaluate foundation models
body: Claude has emerged as the preferred foundation model for building autonomous agents, with developers across platforms reporting significant velocity and cost-effectiveness compared to alternatives like Grok. For healthcare organizations, this matters because clinical AI workflows, robotic process automation in EHR systems, and autonomous agents handling prior authorization and documentation increasingly depend on which foundation model powers them. Healthcare CIOs and AI consultants should evaluate whether Claude's demonstrated performance in agent tasks translates to measurable improvements in clinical workflow automation, documentation accuracy, and total cost of ownership for AI implementations. The broader vendor landscape is consolidating around specific foundation models for agent use cases, which will influence strategic decisions about build-versus-buy approaches for healthcare AI infrastructure.
